response.justification: Strong, versatile full-stack engineer with ~13 years of experience, a CS degree, exposure to high-throughput microservices, and genuine engineering-craft credentials (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring). However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position centered on growth, experimentation, and measurement platforms, and the resume shows no experimentation/A-B testing, feature-flagging, telemetry, or statistical-analysis platform work, nor demonstrated principal-level cross-team architectural leadership at enterprise scale. The fit is partial: solid generalist foundations but a clear gap against the core domain and seniority signals.
strengths: 13+ years across web, mobile, embedded; CS degree and broad language exposure (C/C++, Java, C#, JS/TS)
Real high-scale, low-latency microservices experience (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring/teaching
concerns: No experimentation, feature-flagging, A/B testing, or measurement-platform experience — the core of the role
Limited evidence of Principal/IC6-level cross-team architecture and influence at enterprise scale
Little demonstrated data/statistical-analysis or telemetry-infrastructure depth
